countries.name: aw
countries.flag:
The flag of Aruba consists of a light blue background with two narrow parallel yellow stripes near the bottom and a four-pointed red star outlined in white in the upper left-side corner. The blue color represents the sky and sea surrounding the island, while the yellow stripes symbolize the sun and the island's status as part of the Kingdom of the Netherlands. The red star with its white outline represents the four cardinal directions and also stands for the island itself, which is known as "One Happy Island" due to its beautiful beaches, friendly people, and relaxed atmosphere. The proportions of the flag are 2:3.

The flag of Aruba was created on March 18, 1976. It was designed by a local Aruban student who won a national competition to design the new flag. The competition was organized by the Aruban government in anticipation of the country's separation from the Netherlands Antilles and the establishment of its own autonomous status within the Kingdom of the Netherlands.
countries.description: Aruba is a constituent country of the Kingdom of the Netherlands located in the mid-south of the Caribbean Sea, north of Venezuela and northwest of Curaçao. It is part of the ABC islands and has a dry climate and arid landscape. Aruba is one of four countries that form the Kingdom of the Netherlands and has a population of approximately 108,000 people and a total area of 179 square kilometers. The official languages are Dutch and Papiamento, and the capital is Oranjestad. The country's economy is largely dependent on tourism, accounting for more than 90% of the GDP, with additional industries including oil refining, financial services, and transport. The climate is warm and sunny throughout the year, and the landscape is characterized by a dry, arid environment.

countries.name: af
countries.flag:
The flag of Afghanistan features three equal vertical stripes of black, red, and green, with the national emblem in white at the center. The emblem consists of a mosque with a pulpit and flags on either side, below which are Eastern Arabic numerals for the solar year 1298 (1919 in the Gregorian calendar, the year of Afghan independence from the UK) and an inscription that reads "God is Great" in Arabic.The black stripe represents the dark past of Afghanistan, while the red stripe symbolizes the blood shed for independence, and the green stripe represents hope for a bright future. The white emblem in the center represents Afghanistan's Muslim faith and its commitment to independence, peace, and stability. The proportions of the flag are 2:3.

The current flag of Afghanistan has been in use since 2004 and it's based on the flag used during the monarchy period, following the removal of the Taliban regime. Since Afghanistan gained independence from British colonial rule in 1919, the flag has undergone several changes in response to political events and changes in government.
countries.description:
Afghanistan is a landlocked country located in South Asia and Central Asia. It shares borders with Pakistan to the east and south, Iran to the west, Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan, and Tajikistan to the north, and China to the northeast. The country has a rich history, which can be traced back to the early civilizations of the region. Over the centuries, Afghanistan has been a crossroads of different cultures, religions, and civilizations, which have left their marks on the country's history and culture.

Afghanistan is known for its rugged mountain ranges, vast deserts, and fertile valleys. The country has a diverse landscape that includes the Hindu Kush mountains, the Pamir Mountains, the Amu Darya river, and the Khyber Pass. These natural features have made Afghanistan an important strategic location throughout history, as it sits at the crossroads of different regions and civilizations.

Afghanistan is a multi-ethnic and multilingual country, with Pashtuns and Tajiks being the largest ethnic groups. Other major ethnic groups include Hazaras, Uzbeks, and Turkmen. The country's official languages are Pashto and Dari.

countries.name: ao
countries.flag:
The flag of Angola features two horizontal stripes, one red and one black, with a large emblem in the center. The emblem consists of a yellow half gear wheel crossed by a machete with a star above it. The red stripe at the top of the flag represents the blood shed by Angolans during their struggle for independence from Portugal, while the black stripe at the bottom represents the continent of Africa. The emblem in the center represents the workers and peasants of Angola, as well as the country's industry, agriculture, and natural resources. The yellow color of the emblem represents the country's mineral wealth, and the stars symbolize international solidarity with Africa and the socialist principles that guided Angola's independence movement. The proportions of the flag are 2:3.

The flag of Angola was first adopted on November 11, 1975, when the country gained independence from Portugal. Since then, it has undergone several changes to reflect the country's political and ideological shifts. The current version of the flag was adopted in 1992, following the end of the Angolan Civil War.
countries.description:
Angola is a country located in southwestern Africa. It is the seventh-largest country on the continent and has a population of approximately 32 million people. Angola gained independence from Portugal in 1975 and has since experienced a tumultuous history, including a civil war that lasted from 1975 to 2002.

Angola is rich in natural resources, including oil and diamonds, but poverty and inequality are still significant issues. The country is diverse, with over 90 ethnic groups and many languages spoken, but Portuguese is still the official language.

Angola's landscape is diverse, with a coastline stretching over 1,650 kilometers, vast savannas, and tropical rainforests. The country is also home to a variety of wildlife, including elephants, lions, and giraffes. Angola has a developing tourism industry, with attractions such as the Kalandula Falls, the Iona National Park, and the Tunda Vala Fissure.

countries.name: ai
countries.flag:
The flag of Anguilla is a British Blue Ensign, which is a variation of the British flag that is commonly used by British Overseas Territories. The flag features a blue field with the Union Jack in the upper left corner and the Anguillan coat of arms in the right-hand side of the flag. he coat of arms features three interlocking dolphins, which represent friendship, wisdom, and strength, as well as the unity of the island's people. The dolphins are set over a light blue background, which represents the surrounding Caribbean Sea. The proportions of the flag are 1:2.

The current version of the flag of Anguilla was created in 1990, when the island was granted a new constitution and officially became a separate British Overseas Territory.
countries.description:
Anguilla is a small island territory located in the Caribbean Sea. It is one of the British Overseas Territories, and is located east of Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands. The island covers an area of approximately 91 square kilometers, and has a population of around 15,000 people.

Anguilla is known for its beautiful beaches, warm hospitality, and crystal-clear waters, making it a popular tourist destination. Its economy is largely based on tourism, as well as offshore banking and finance. The island's main town and capital is The Valley, which is located on the northern coast.

countries.name: al
countries.flag:
The flag of Albania is a simple design consisting of two main elements, a red background and a black, double-headed eagle in the center. The red background is a symbol of bravery, strength, and valor, while the eagle represents the Albanian people's national identity, freedom, and sovereignty. The eagle is facing both left and right, representing the country's ability to defend itself from threats from both sides. The proportions of the flag are 5:7.

The design of the flag is based on a popular legend about the founder of the Albanian nation, Skanderbeg, who fought against the Ottoman Empire in the 15th century. The first modern Albanian flag was raised in Vlore on November 28, 1912, during the country's declaration of independence from the Ottoman Empire. Over the years, the design of the Albanian flag has undergone some changes, particularly during the communist era when the national emblem was added to the flag. However, the current design is a return to the traditional design used before.
countries.description:
Albania is a small country located in southeastern Europe, bordered by Montenegro to the northwest, Kosovo to the northeast, North Macedonia to the east, Greece to the south and the Adriatic Sea and the Ionian Sea to the west. The country has a population of approximately 2.9 million people and covers an area of 28,748 square kilometers. The capital and largest city of Albania is Tirana, which is also the country's political and cultural center. Other major cities in Albania include Durres, Vlore, Shkoder, and Elbasan.

Albania has a rich history and culture with influences from its Greek, Roman, Byzantine, Ottoman, and communist past. The Albanian economy is largely based on agriculture, with some small-scale manufacturing and services industries. Albania is known for its stunning natural beauty, warm hospitality, and delicious Mediterranean and Balcan cuisine.

countries.name: ax
countries.flag:
The flag of Åland, also known as the Åland Islands, is a yellow or gold Nordic cross with another red cross inside on a blue background with the vertical bar shifted towards the hoist side. This flag is intended to resemble the Swedish flag defaced by a red cross symbolizing Finland, as the islands are located between both countries. The proportions of the flag are 17:26.

The flag was officially adopted as the flag of Åland in 1954 and first hoisted in Mariehamn on 3 April 1954. Prior to autonomy, an unofficial horizontal bicolour triband of blue-yellow-blue was in use until it was made illegal in 1935.
countries.description:
The Åland Islands (sometimes also referred to as Åland) are a group of about 6,700 islands located in the Baltic Sea between Sweden and Finland. The archipelago has a total land area of about 1,580 square kilometers, with most of the islands being very small and uninhabited. Of the inhabited islands, the largest are Fasta Åland (the main island) and Kökar.

The islands have a population of about 30,000 people, who are primarily Swedish-speaking. The official languages are Swedish and Finnish, and the islands have a high degree of autonomy within Finland. The capital city of Åland is Mariehamn, which is located on the main island.

The Åland Islands have a long and complex history, having been ruled by Sweden, Russia, and Finland at different times. The islands are known for their natural beauty, with picturesque coastal villages, rocky shores, and lush forests. Tourism is an important industry, as is shipping due to the archipelago's strategic location. The islands also have a unique culture and identity, with strong ties to Scandinavia and a distinctively Swedish-influenced way of life.

countries.name: ad
countries.flag:
The national flag of the Principality of Andorra features a vertical tricolour of blue, yellow, and red with the coat of arms of Andorra in the center. Blue and red are the colors of France, yellow and red are those of Spain, and together they reflect Franco-Spanish protection. The coat of arms combines the arms of the bishopric of Urgel, the counts of Foix, Catalonia, and Bearn. The proportions of the flag are 7:10.

The historical flag of Andorra had two red and yellow stripes. In 1866, a variant of the modern flag without the coat of arms was designed, adding a blue stripe. From 1931 to 1939, a horizontal version of the flag was used to reflect the Spanish flag. From 1939 to 1959, different versions of the flag were used until the modern flag was standardized in 1993 with Andorra's entry into the United Nations.
countries.description:
Andorra is a small landlocked country located in the eastern Pyrenees mountains between France and Spain. With a total area of just 468 square kilometers, it is one of the smallest countries in Europe. Andorra has a population of around 77,000 people, making it one of the least populous countries in the world.

The economy of Andorra is heavily based on tourism and finance. The country is known for its ski resorts, natural beauty, and duty-free shopping. Andorra also has a well-developed banking sector, and is known as a tax haven. The official language is Catalan, although Spanish, French, and Portuguese are also widely spoken.

Andorra has a unique political system. It is a parliamentary co-principality, meaning that it is ruled jointly by two co-princes: the Bishop of Urgell in Catalonia, Spain, and the President of France.

countries.name: ae
countries.flag:
The flag of the United Arab Emirates (UAE) consists of three equal horizontal bands of green, white, and black, with a vertical red stripe on the hoist side. These flag's four colors represent the country's commitment to its cultural and Islamic heritage, its economic development, its peaceful coexistence with other nations, and its determination to defend its sovereignty and unity. The proportions of the flag are 1:2.

The UAE flag was designed by a young Emirati, Mr. Abdulla Mohammed Al Ma'enah, after he saw an advertisement for a competition to design the country's flag. The competition was sponsored by the Al Diwan Al Amiri (the Ruler's office) in Abu Dhabi.
countries.description:
The United Arab Emirates (UAE) is a country located in the southeastern part of the Arabian Peninsula in Western Asia. The country is made up of seven Emirates. Each Emirate has its own ruler, but they are all united under a federal system of government.

The UAE has a diverse and multicultural population, with the majority of the population being expatriates from various countries, including India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Egypt, and the Philippines. Arabic is the official language of the country, but English is widely spoken and understood. The UAE is also known for its cultural heritage and traditional Islamic values. However, it is also a modern and cosmopolitan country that is open to other cultures and ideas.

It is also known for its vast oil reserves, which have helped fuel its rapid economic development over the past few decades and has turned it to be one of the most prosperous and technologically advanced countries. However, the country has also diversified its economy and invested heavily in tourism, real estate, and other industries to reduce its dependence on oil.

countries.name: ar
countries.flag:
The flag of Argentina is made up of three horizontal stripes of equal width, with the national emblem in the center of the middle stripe. The top and bottom stripes are light blue and it is said that it represent freedom, military cockades, and the blue sky over the battlefield, while the middle stripe is white and represents clouds. The sun or emblem in the center of the flag is called the "Sun of May" and it symbolizes the sun's presence during the May Revolution of 1810, which led to Argentina's independence from Spain. The proportions of the flag are 5:8 or 9:14.

The flag of Argentina was designed by General Manuel Belgrano during the Argentine War of Independence, and it was first hoisted on February 27, 1812, in Rosario, Argentina.
countries.description:
Argentina is a large country located in the southern part of South America. The large land area of the territory covers approximately 2.78 million square kilometers. It has a diverse landscape and that includes the Andes Mountains, vast grasslands known as the pampas, and the southern tip of South America known as Tierra del Fuego.

Argentina is a multicultural country, with a population of approximately 45 million people. The official language is Spanish, and the majority of the population is of European descent, primarily of Italian and Spanish ancestry.

Argentina has a mixed economy that is heavily dependent on exports of agricultural products, such as soybeans, wheat, and beef. The country is also rich in natural resources, including minerals such as copper, gold, and lithium. Tourism is also an important industry in Argentina, as it's home to world-class attraction such as the capital itself, Buenos Aires. In addition to tourism, soccer is another significant industry in Argentina, with millions of fans both in the country and around the world.

countries.name: as
countries.flag:
The flag of American Samoa has a dark blue background with a red-edged white triangle pointing towards the hoist side. Within the triangle there is a bald eagle facing the hoist side clutching a war club and fly-whisk known as the known as the "talking stick". They symbolizes the wisdom of Samoa's traditional chiefs, and the power of the state. The blue represents freedom and red symbolizing courage and strength. The proportions of the flag are 1:2.

In 1959, Samoana High School held a flag design contest for its students. The winner's flag, 19-year-old Rapi Sotoa's flag, was officially adopted April 17, 1960.
countries.description:
American Samoa is a territory of the United States located in the South Pacific Ocean. It consists of five volcanic islands and two coral atolls, with a total land area of about 76 square miles (197 square kilometers). The territory has a tropical climate with high humidity and rainfall throughout the year. The population of American Samoa is around 55,000, and the official languages are English and Samoan.

The economy is mainly based on tuna canning, agriculture, and tourism. The culture of American Samoa is deeply rooted in Polynesian traditions, with strong emphasis on family, community, and respect for elders. The territory is also known for its traditional dance, music, and sports, including the Samoan version of rugby known as "Manu Samoa".

countries.name: aq
countries.flag:
The flag of Antarctica does not represent any sovereign nation or government, as Antarctica is not a country. However, it is commonly used to represent the continent as a whole. The flag features a blue background with a white map of Antarctica in the center. The map depicts the continent without any national boundaries, symbolizing Antarctica as a neutral and international territory by using the same colors as the flag of the United Nations.

The flag was first proposed by a British scientist named Graham Colin Owen in 1986, and it was officially adopted by the Antarctic Treaty Consultative Meeting (ATCM) in 2002. The flag is often used by Antarctic research stations and expeditions as a symbol of their connection to the continent. There are several other proposals but Graham's version has been used most in general.
countries.description:
Antarctica is situated at the South Pole and is surrounded by the Southern Ocean. It has a total area of about 14 million square kilometers. The continent is covered by a massive ice sheet which is several kilometers thick in some places and holds about 90% of the world's freshwater. Precipitation is very limited and mostly occurs in the form of snow. Despite its harsh environment, Antarctica is home to a surprising variety of wildlife.

Antarctica is governed by the Antarctic Treaty System, a collection of international agreements that regulate human activity on the continent. The treaty prohibits military activity, mineral mining, and nuclear testing. It also designates Antarctica as a scientific preserve, emphasizing environmental protection and international cooperation in research efforts.

countries.name: tf
countries.flag:
The French Southern and Antarctic Lands flag consists of a blue field with the French national emblem, known as the "tricolor", in the canton. This emblem represents the connection of the French Southern and Antarctic Lands to France as an overseas territory. In the lower fly, the letters T.A.A.F (from the French name Terres Australes et Antarctiques Françaises) forms a monogram in white, which is stylized to resemble an anchor. The monogram is surrounded by five white stars.

In 1958, the first senior administrator of the territory, Xavier Richert, introduced a flag for his office which was nearly dentical to the current version with the exception of having three stars instead of five. The current version was adopted in 2007.
countries.description: The French Southern and Antarctic Lands is a group of overseas territories administered by France. It encompasses several islands in the southern Indian Ocean and sections of Antarctica. The territory is uninhabited for the most part due to harsh weather and conditions, with the exception of temporary research station personnel. These territories serve as important sites for scientific research, conservation efforts, and monitoring of the environment. They are governed by the French government and are subject to strict regulations to protect their unique ecosystems and maintain their scientific value.

countries.name: ag
countries.flag:
The flag of Antigua and Barbuda consists of a horizontal tri-colour of black, blue (half-width), and white, with two red right scalene triangles on opposite sides. On the black band is a yellow half-sun with nine rays. The red part symbolises lifeblood of slave forefathers and dynamism of the people, whereas blues symbolises hope and black represents African heritage. At the same time the yellow, blue, and white colors symbolize the islands main tourist attractions: Sun, sea, and sand.

The flag of Antigua and Barbuda was adopted in 1967 when these two islands in the Caribbean Sea became independent from Great Britain. The design of the flag was created by Antiguan teacher Reginald Samuels and chosen in a national flag design competition. The flag should refer to the past but also to better prospects for the future.
countries.description:
Antigua and Barbuda is a sovereign nation located in the Caribbean Sea, consisting of the islands of Antigua, Barbuda, and smaller islets. Antigua is the larger and more populated one out of the two main islands and it's situated to the south of Barbuda.

The majority of the population is of African descent. Most of Antigua’s inhabitants live in St. John’s. The language is English, and some two-thirds of the people are Christian. Antigua and Barbuda enjoy a tropical climate with warm temperatures year-round. Thanks to this, along with its stunning beaches, rich history, warm climate, and welcoming culture, the islands provide a picturesque and inviting destination for travelers seeking a Caribbean getaway.

countries.name: au
countries.flag:
The Australian flag features a dark blue background with the Union Jack in the upper hoist-side corner, representing Australia's historical connection to Britain. Below the Union Jack is a large white seven-pointed star called the Commonwealth Star, symbolizing the federation of the original six Australian states, and the seventh tip was added to refers to future independent federal territories. The star represents unity and equality among Australia's states and territories.

On the right side of the flag, there is the Southern Cross constellation, consisting of five white stars, which holds cultural and navigational significance for Australia. The flag represents Australia's history, national unity, and its position in the Southern Hemisphere.

The Australian Flag was flown for the first time on 3 September 1901, the day on which the then Prime Minister announced the 5 joint competition winners.
countries.description:
Australia is the world's sixth-largest country by total area, spanning approximately 7.7 million square kilometers. It is surrounded by the Indian and Pacific Oceans and is the only country that encompasses an entire continent. Australia is divided into six states and two major mainland territories. The capital city is Canberra altough the largest city is Sydney. Australia has a population of approximately 26 million people. The country is known for its multicultural society, with a rich mix of cultural backgrounds. Indigenous Australians still have a big influence in the culture, arts, sports, and recreation.

Australia has one of the world's largest developed economies and is known for its abundance of natural resources, including coal, iron ore, gold, and natural gas. Due to its large size, Australia provides a wide range of landscapes and climates. Australia is famous for its unique and diverse wildlife. It is home to various iconic animals such as kangaroos, koalas, platypus, wombats, and emus. The Great Barrier Reef, located off the coast of Queensland, is the world's largest coral reef system and supports a vast array of marine life.

countries.flag: The Spanish flag consists of three horizontal stripes in red, yellow, and red colors, with the yellow stripe being twice the size of each red stripe. In the center of the yellow stripe is the national coat of arms, which features the Pillars of Hercules, a crowned shield, and other symbolic elements. The colors and design of the flag are based on the centuries-old heraldic traditions of Spain. The proportions of the flag are 2:3.
countries.description:
Spain is a country located in southwestern Europe on the Iberian Peninsula. It has a diverse geography that includes the Pyrenees Mountains, the Meseta Plateau, and a long coastline along the Mediterranean Sea, the Atlantic Ocean, and the Bay of Biscay. The capital and largest city of Spain is Madrid.

Spain has a rich cultural heritage that is shaped by its history of being a melting pot of various civilizations. It has been inhabited by different groups of people over the centuries, including the Iberians, Celts, Romans, Visigoths, Moors, and others. As a result, Spain's culture, language, and traditions are influenced by this diverse mix of influences.

Spain is also famous for its vibrant and colorful festivals, such as La Tomatina, the Running of the Bulls, and the Feria de Abril. These festivals are an essential part of Spain's culture and attract millions of tourists every year.

Spain is the world's fourteenth-largest economy and a member of the European Union. It has a well-developed tourism industry and is also known for its agriculture, wine, and fashion industries. Spain is also a popular destination for expats and retirees due to its high quality of life, warm climate, and low cost of living.
